description: Fix Ralph process survival: SIGHUP handling, persistent log files, enhanced ralph_start with log redirection, enriched ralph_status with PID/uptime/logs, and new ralph_logs MCP tool.

userStories.title: Add SIGHUP handler to run.js
userStories.description: Add process.on('SIGHUP', () => {}) in the run() function to ignore SIGHUP signals, so the Ralph loop survives terminal/SSH disconnects. Must be added alongside existing SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: SIGHUP is ignored (empty handler) so process does not terminate
Existing SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown handlers are preserved

userStories.title: Persistent log file for detached ralph_start
userStories.description: Modify ralph_start in server.js to redirect stdout/stderr to a log file (.ralph/logs/ralph-loop.log) instead of stdio:'ignore'. Use openSync to create file descriptors and pass them to spawn's stdio option. Create the logs directory if it doesn't exist.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph_start spawns with stdio redirected to .ralph/logs/ralph-loop.log
Log file is created/appended (not truncated) on each start
Logs directory is created if missing
The spawned process remains detached and unref'd

userStories.title: Enrich ralph_status with PID, uptime, and log tail
userStories.description: Enhance getCompactStatus() in status.js and the ralph_status MCP handler to include: running PID (from .ralph/.lock or status.txt), process uptime, last story processed, and last 5 lines of ralph-loop.log. Check if PID is still alive using process.kill(pid, 0).
acceptanceCriteria: ralph_status returns pid field (or null if not running)
ralph_status returns lastLogLines array (last 5 lines of ralph-loop.log)

userStories.title: Add ralph_logs MCP tool
userStories.description: Add a new MCP tool 'ralph_logs' that reads the last N lines (default 20) from .ralph/logs/ralph-loop.log. Parameters: lines (number, default 20). Returns the log content as text. Handle missing log file gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: New ralph_logs tool is registered in TOOLS array
Returns helpful message if log file does not exist

userStories.title: Store PID in lock file for process tracking
userStories.description: Modify the lock file mechanism in config.js to write the PID to .ralph/.lock instead of just creating an empty file. Update acquireLock() to write process.pid, and add a method isLoopRunning() that reads the PID from .lock and checks if it's alive. Update ralph_start to also write the child PID to a .ralph/.pid file for the detached case.
acceptanceCriteria: New isLoopRunning() method reads PID and checks with kill(pid,0)
